engineIntelligence.h2: Aston Martin Vantage Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Aston Martin Vantage in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: Aston Martin Vantage AJ37 engine - petrol 4.7L replacement UK
knownFailures: Timing chain tensioner rattle - premature wear causes chain noise and potential failure from 60k miles.
High oil consumption - piston ring wear leads to excessive oil burn on high-mileage units.
VVT actuator failure - variable valve timing components degrade, affecting performance and idle.

cards.altText: Aston Martin Vantage AM28 engine - petrol 5.9L replacement UK
knownFailures: Cooling system failure - radiator and water pump issues cause overheating on high-mileage V12 units.
Oil leak from cam covers - precision sealing degradation leads to external oil seepage.
Dry-sump lubrication wear - scavenge pump components require specialist inspection.

cards.altText: Aston Martin Vantage M177 engine - petrol 4.0L replacement UK
knownFailures: Turbocharger failure - twin-turbo units can develop boost leaks or bearing wear from 40k miles.
Intercooler heat soak - charge cooling efficiency drops under sustained high-load driving.
ECU calibration drift - software updates required to maintain optimal performance mapping.

engineGuide.h2: Aston Martin Vantage Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Aston Martin Vantage across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 4.3L Petrol
entries.history: Early naturally aspirated V8 powering first-generation Vantage; aluminium block with traditional Aston character.
compatibleVariants: V8 Vantage Coupe, V8 Vantage Roadster
commonFailures: Oil consumption from piston ring wear - typically evident after 70k miles
Timing chain guide degradation - causes rattle and potential chain jump
Variable valve timing solenoid failure - affects idle quality and throttle response
entries.cta: Get quotes for AJ-V8 Aston Martin Vantage engine replacement

entries.title: 4.7L Petrol
entries.history: Upgraded 4.7L evolution of AJ-V8; most common Vantage engine with improved power and refinement.
compatibleVariants: V8 Vantage Coupe, V8 Vantage Roadster, V8 Vantage S, V8 Vantage N420, V8 Vantage N430
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner rattle - premature wear from 60k miles requiring preventative replacement
High oil consumption - piston ring and bore wear on high-mileage examples
VVT actuator failure - cam phasing components degrade affecting performance mapping
entries.cta: Get quotes for AJ37 Aston Martin Vantage engine replacement

entries.title: 5.9L Petrol
entries.history: Naturally aspirated V12 derived from DBS; collector-grade powertrain with dry-sump lubrication for track capability.
compatibleVariants: V12 Vantage Coupe, V12 Vantage Roadster, V12 Vantage S, V12 Vantage GT12
commonFailures: Cooling system failure - radiator and water pump issues cause overheating under load
Oil leak from cam cover seals - precision gasket degradation on complex V12 layout
Dry-sump scavenge pump wear - requires specialist inspection on high-mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for AM28 Aston Martin Vantage engine replacement

entries.title: 4.0L Petrol
entries.history: Mercedes-AMG twin-turbo V8 bringing modern forced induction performance to new-generation Vantage.
compatibleVariants: New Vantage Coupe, New Vantage Roadster, Vantage F1 Edition
commonFailures: Turbocharger bearing wear - twin-turbo units can develop boost leaks from 40k miles
Intercooler heat soak - charge cooling efficiency drops under sustained high-load driving
ECU calibration drift - software updates required to maintain optimal performance mapping
entries.cta: Get quotes for M177 Aston Martin Vantage engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Aston Martin Vantage Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Aston Martin Vantage's naturally aspirated V8 and V12 engines, plus the newer AMG M177 twin-turbo, each have documented failure patterns that drive UK replacement enquiries. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.group: V8 Vantage Timing Chain Tensioner Failure
problems.h4: V8 Vantage Timing Chain Tensioner Failure - Why AJ37 Engines Develop Rattle
problems.affectedModels: Aston Martin Vantage V8 Coupe, Roadster, S, N420, N430 (2009-2017, AJ37 4.7L)
problems.rootCause: Hydraulic tensioner loses pressure over time, allowing timing chain slack that creates metallic rattle on cold start and can lead to chain jump or valve damage if ignored.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace tensioner, guides and chain; reset timing
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught before chain damage; 40-60k mile extension

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with upgraded tensioners, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Restores factory performance; recommended for high-mileage or damaged units

problems.recommendation: If you hear cold-start rattle below 70k miles, a preventative tensioner and chain kit from a V8 specialist offers best value. Beyond 80k miles or if performance has dropped, a rebuilt AJ37 with upgraded components is the more reliable long-term investment.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt AJ37 engine prices for your Aston Martin Vantage

problems.h4: V12 Vantage Cooling System Failure - Preventing Overheating Damage
problems.affectedModels: Aston Martin Vantage V12 Coupe, Roadster, S, GT12 (2009-2018, AM28 5.9L)
problems.rootCause: Plastic radiator end tanks and water pump impellers degrade with heat cycles; failure causes coolant loss and rapid overheating that can warp cylinder heads.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace radiator, water pump, thermostat, hoses and coolant; pressure test
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses common failure points; suitable if no overheating damage occurred

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt V12 with upgraded cooling components, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Essential if head warp or bearing damage suspected; preserves collector value

problems.recommendation: On a V12 Vantage worth £40k-£80k, proactive cooling system renewal at 60k miles is far cheaper than engine replacement. If overheating has occurred, insist on a compression test before committing to repairs.
problems.cta: Get quotes for AM28 V12 engine replacement for your Aston Martin Vantage

problems.h4: New Vantage M177 Turbocharger Failure - Twin-Turbo Reliability
problems.affectedModels: Aston Martin Vantage Coupe, Roadster, F1 Edition (2018-present, M177 4.0L)
problems.rootCause: Turbo bearing seals degrade under high heat; oil contamination or delayed servicing accelerates wear, causing boost loss, smoke or catastrophic turbo seizure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace failed turbo(s), update ECU calibration, flush oil system
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if failure caught early with no engine damage; 30-50k mile expectation

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt AMG V8 with upgraded turbo seals, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if oil contamination occurred or for high-mileage performance preservation

problems.recommendation: M177 turbos respond well to specialist rebuild with upgraded seals. If oil was contaminated or mileage exceeds 50k, a full rebuilt engine with warranty offers better long-term security for a performance asset.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt M177 engine prices for your Aston Martin Vantage

problems.group: V8/V12 Oil Consumption & Piston Ring Wear
problems.h4: V8/V12 Oil Consumption & Piston Ring Wear - Restoration Options
problems.affectedModels: Aston Martin Vantage V8 (AJ-V8/AJ37) and V12 (AM28) (2005-2018)
problems.rootCause: Piston ring and bore wear allows oil into combustion chambers; symptoms include blue exhaust smoke, fouled plugs and declining compression.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder heads, replace rings, valves, guides and seals; hone bores
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if bottom end is sound; restores compression and reduces oil burn

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit fully rebuilt unit with upgraded rings/bearings, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage examples or if bottom-end wear is suspected

problems.recommendation: A compression and leak-down test determines if a top-end rebuild suffices. For Vantages over 80k miles, a full rebuilt engine with warranty often proves more cost-effective than incremental repairs.
problems.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt Aston Martin Vantage engines with upgraded components

problems.h4: VVT Actuator & Cam Phaser Failure - Performance Restoration
problems.affectedModels: Aston Martin Vantage V8 (AJ37) and V12 (AM28) (2009-2018)
problems.rootCause: Hydraulic VVT actuators lose sealing pressure; symptoms include rough idle, reduced power, check engine lights and timing-related fault codes.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace faulty actuators, update ECU calibration, reset adaptations
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if failure is isolated; restores throttle response and idle quality

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with upgraded VVT components, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if multiple actuators failed or engine has high mileage

problems.recommendation: VVT failures are often isolated. Start with diagnostic scanning to confirm which bank is affected; actuator replacement by a Vantage specialist typically resolves the issue without full engine replacement.
problems.cta: Compare Aston Martin Vantage VVT repair vs replacement costs

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Aston Martin Vantage
finalCta.paragraph: From timing chain rattle on the V8 to cooling failures on the V12 and turbo wear on the new AMG, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Aston Martin Vantage specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare quotes today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Aston Martin Vantage Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Aston Martin Vantage and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineYears.h2: Aston Martin Vantage Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This timeline covers all major engine changes across the Aston Martin Vantage's UK production history, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from specialists.

years.description: The V8 Vantage debuted with the 4.3L AJ-V8, establishing the model's naturally aspirated character.
keyChanges: Launch of first-generation V8 Vantage Coupe (2005)
AJ-V8 4.3L naturally aspirated V8 as sole powertrain
knownFor: Traditional Aston throttle response and exhaust note
Oil consumption becomes evident after 70k miles
Timing chain guide wear on high-mileage examples
Strong enthusiast following for early manual examples
years.cta: Get quotes for 2005-2008 Aston Martin Vantage replacement engines
years.ticker: V8 Vantage oil consumption fix • AJ-V8 timing chain rattle • V8 Vantage engine rebuild cost

years.description: The upgraded 4.7L AJ37 V8 and new 5.9L AM28 V12 expanded the Vantage range with more power and collector appeal.
keyChanges: AJ37 4.7L V8 replaces AJ-V8 across V8 range (2009)
V12 Vantage Coupe launched with AM28 engine (2009)
V8 Vantage S introduced with 430hp calibration (2011)
Limited editions N420 (2012) and N430 (2015) released
V12 Roadster (2012), V12 S (2016) and GT12 (2015) added
knownFor: AJ37 timing chain tensioner rattle is most common failure
V12 cooling system requires proactive maintenance
N430 represents peak development of AJ37 platform
GT12 is ultra-rare track-focused collector variant
years.cta: Get quotes for 2009-2017 Aston Martin Vantage replacement engines
years.ticker: AJ37 timing chain replacement • V12 Vantage overheating fix • V8 Vantage S engine rebuild cost

years.description: The all-new Vantage platform launched with Mercedes-AMG's 4.0L twin-turbo M177 V8, bringing modern performance technology.
keyChanges: All-new Vantage platform with AMG M177 powertrain (2018)
Roadster variant added to new generation (2020)
F1 Edition limited model with 528hp calibration (2021)
mainEngines: M177 - 4.0L twin-turbo petrol (New Vantage Coupe/Roadster/F1 Edition)
knownFor: Twin-turbo performance with modern thermal management
Turbo reliability depends on strict oil service discipline
ECU calibration updates essential for optimal mapping
Strong demand for rebuilt M177 units as first replacements emerge
years.cta: Get quotes for 2018-present Aston Martin Vantage replacement engines
years.ticker: M177 turbo failure fix • New Vantage engine replacement cost • F1 Edition engine rebuild

faq.h2: Aston Martin Vantage Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does an Aston Martin Vantage engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Rebuilt Aston Martin Vantage engines typically range from £4,500 for a V8 AJ37 to £22,000 for a V12 AM28, with new-generation M177 units from £6,500. Prices vary by engine code, variant and supplier.
keyPoints: V8 AJ37 rebuilt: £4,500-£12,500 supply only
V12 AM28 rebuilt: £8,500-£22,000 supply only
M177 AMG rebuilt: £6,500-£18,500 supply only
Supply & fit labour adds £1,200-£4,500 typically
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Aston Martin Vantage engine prices for your variant

items.question: What is the most common engine problem on the V8 Vantage?
items.answer: Timing chain tensioner rattle on the 4.7L AJ37 V8 is the most frequently reported issue, typically appearing between 60,000-90,000 miles and requiring preventative replacement.
keyPoints: Cold-start metallic rattle indicates tensioner wear
Ignoring can lead to chain jump and valve damage
Preventative kit replacement costs £950-£1,800 specialist
Rebuilt AJ37 with upgraded tensioners: £4,500-£12,500
items.cta: Get quotes for AJ37 timing chain preventative replacement

items.question: Is the V12 Vantage engine reliable for high mileage?
items.answer: The AM28 V12 is robust when cooling systems are maintained, but plastic radiator components and water pumps commonly fail between 50,000-80,000 miles requiring proactive replacement.
keyPoints: Cooling system overhaul recommended at 60k miles
Dry-sump lubrication requires specialist servicing
Oil leaks from cam covers are common but manageable
Rebuilt AM28 with upgraded cooling: £8,500-£22,000
items.cta: Compare V12 Vantage engine replacement options with warranty

items.question: Can I upgrade my V8 Vantage to V12 specification?
items.answer: While technically possible, converting a V8 Vantage to V12 requires extensive chassis, cooling, ECU and drivetrain modifications costing £30,000+ and is rarely cost-effective versus buying a factory V12.
keyPoints: Conversion requires custom mounts, wiring and calibration
Cooling, brakes and suspension need significant upgrades
Resale value rarely recovers conversion investment
Rebuilt V8 AJ37 offers better value for performance gains
items.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt V8 Vantage engines with performance calibration

items.question: How long does a rebuilt Aston Martin Vantage engine last?
items.answer: A properly rebuilt Vantage engine with upgraded components addressing known failure points should deliver 40,000-60,000 miles of reliable service when maintained to specialist specifications.
keyPoints: Upgraded timing components extend AJ37 lifespan significantly
Strict oil service intervals critical for M177 turbo longevity
V12 dry-sump systems require specialist fluid specifications
12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard on rebuilt units
items.cta: Find rebuilt Aston Martin Vantage engines with upgraded failure-point components

items.question: What warranty comes with a reconditioned Vantage engine?
items.answer: Engines sourced through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from vetted UK specialists, with some suppliers offering extended coverage for collector variants.
keyPoints: Minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard
Warranty covers parts and labour at supplying specialist
GT12 and F1 Edition may qualify for extended terms
Always confirm exact warranty scope before purchase
items.cta: Compare Aston Martin Vantage engine suppliers by warranty terms

items.question: Should I rebuild or replace my Vantage engine?
items.answer: For Vantages under 70k miles with isolated failures, a targeted rebuild often offers best value; beyond 80k miles or with multiple issues, a full rebuilt engine with warranty is typically more cost-effective long-term.
keyPoints: Compression and leak-down tests inform rebuild viability
High-mileage AJ37/AM28 benefit from full rebuild economics
M177 turbo failures often warrant full engine replacement
Factor in vehicle value and intended ownership period
items.cta: Get expert advice on Aston Martin Vantage rebuild vs replace decision

items.question: Can a Vantage engine run on modern unleaded and E10 fuel?
items.answer: All Aston Martin Vantage engines are designed for unleaded petrol; E10 compatibility is confirmed for M177 AMG units, while older AJ-V8/AJ37/AM28 engines may benefit from fuel system inspection for ethanol resistance.
keyPoints: M177 AMG engines are E10 compatible from factory
AJ37 and AM28 tolerate E10 but monitor fuel system seals
Older AJ-V8 units may require seal inspection for ethanol
Always use manufacturer-recommended octane rating
items.cta: Enquire about Aston Martin Vantage fuel compatibility upgrades

items.question: How do I identify my Vantage engine code?
items.answer: Your engine code appears on the engine block casting, vehicle VIN decoder, and service records; entering your registration on EngineMarket instantly verifies the exact code and compatible replacement options.
keyPoints: AJ37 stamped on V8 block near timing cover
AM28 visible on V12 intake manifold area
M177 code on AMG turbo housing and ECU
DVLA lookup via registration confirms factory fitment
items.cta: Use our free VIN checker to identify your Aston Martin Vantage engine code

items.question: Is supply & fit available for Vantage engine replacement?
items.answer: Yes, EngineMarket's UK network includes specialists offering full supply & fit service for all Vantage generations, handling removal, installation, calibration and road testing with warranty validation.
keyPoints: Supply & fit labour typically £1,200-£4,500 added cost
Specialists handle ECU calibration and cooling system bleed
Nationwide coverage ensures access to accredited workshops
Post-fit warranty validation included with service
items.cta: Find Aston Martin Vantage engine supply & fit specialists near you
